title: Why were the Nerv scientists killed
display_title: Why were the Nerv scientists killed
wikitext:
[]: Why were the Nerv scientists killed?             Quote Post by Kaworu06 » Sun Dec 23, 2012 12:44 pm So, yeah, I watched EoE, and wondered why every Nerv tech-scientist was killed? I mean, not everyone knew about instrumentality or Rei-Tank. Didn't Seele want everyone to be one, where the flaws of me were fixed by the good of you or vice-versa? It's been a while since I watched EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 23, 2012 12:51 pm The JSSDF had been sent into the HQ of a dangerous cult hell-bent on triggering a replay of Second Impact. Or at least, that's how they would have been briefed.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaworu06 » Sun Dec 23, 2012 12:55 pm Thank you Mr.Tines! I thought Seele said, "Well, just shoot everyone."

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Dec 23, 2012 6:49 pm I wouldn't be too surprised if they didn't really care either way, they didn't seem to be too moved at the idea of having to murder a non-threatening child just because of his (forced) connection to said "cult" at the very least. In fact, the only one who didn't seem to be a sociopath, and who i had incredible amounts of sympathy for considering the short five seconds in which he appears, was the JSSDF commander. "We failed" What a badass.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Dec 23, 2012 8:05 pm Seele doesn't want to take any chances. They don't know what the technicians know or are capable of. And even if they knew nothing, what difference would it make in the long run? Instumentality, whoever's version, will change the nature of existence. They have to eliminate the enemy, regardless of whoever it might be. They need Instrumentality to go their way this time, and the heretical lunatic might initiate it at any time and they can not redo. Dream, who is this child?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Dec 23, 2012 8:17 pm Lavinius wrote: Dream, who is this child?

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Sun Dec 23, 2012 9:37 pm Seele already killed half the worlds population with 2nd Impact. What's a few more on the way to paradise?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Dec 23, 2012 10:23 pm Yeah, this is SEELE. You know, the ones whose evul-ness we're not even debating. They want to make properly sure/ had no further use for them.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Dec 24, 2012 1:14 am Dream wrote: Shinji, when they're going to execute him. "Sorry kid, nothing personal" or something like that. They would have done it too, if it weren't for Misato. Also, Seele most certainly knows what NERV is capable of and the level of threat (or non-threat) the various parts of NERV possess, they just don't care.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 24, 2012 2:13 am One of Anno's influences was the nerve gas attack by the Aum Shinrikyo cult a few months earlier (a significant enough event in Japan that Ikuni was also using it last year). The government being briefed on the group under Tokyo-3 as something along similar lines, only this time armed and with a real Apocalypse toolkit is pretty much a given.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Dec 24, 2012 5:13 am Mr. Tines wrote: One of Anno's influences was the nerve gas attack by the Aum Shinrikyo cult a few months earlier (a significant enough event in Japan that Ikuni was also using it last year). The government being briefed on the group under Tokyo-3 as something along similar lines, only this time armed and with a real Apocalypse toolkit is pretty much a given.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Dec 24, 2012 9:59 am Lavinius wrote: Ah, I thought you were referring to Kaworu or Asuka or something. Shinji is actually quite dangerous, given that he pilots Eva 01 which is quite important to The Gendo Plan. However, it is unlikely that JSSDF knows this. Perhaps the best way to look at these issues is less what Seele knows/wants, and more of what is immediately effective. And that the JSSDF are the ones actually doing the shooting and killing. Perhaps they too are a dangerous cult? I was under the impression that there was some sort of a conflict and secrets between Nerv and Seele? And Nerv isn't an acronym, there's no reason to capitalize it. The same with Seele.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Mon Dec 24, 2012 1:05 pm Darkwing wrote: The Eva pilots would be high priority targets in an attack. Nerv doesn't have much military strength other than the pilots. Even inactive Eva's would be hard to destroy, but no pilots, no problem. As we see even if you cut the power an Eva can do a lot of damage in 5 minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Dec 24, 2012 5:54 pm Darkwing wrote: The Eva pilots would be high priority targets in an attack. Nerv doesn't have much military strength other than the pilots. Even inactive Eva's would be hard to destroy, but no pilots, no problem. As we see even if you cut the power an Eva can do a lot of damage in 5 minutes.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Dec 24, 2012 6:17 pm Dream wrote: What's amusing in that is that if they would have killed Shinji, then it's most likely that everyone would have been irrecuperably fucked. No Shinji, no Unit 01 (maybe), no Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Jan 01, 2013 6:00 pm Seele was more interested in forcing Nerv's hand towards Third Impact than they were in taking hostages. Taking hostages takes time. Killing them is simply an easier (albeit messier) method. As to the debate over whether Shinji was necessary or not, I was divided on the issue until a few days ago, when I took two simple facts into account: 1. All offensive soldiers in Nerv were under Seele's control. 2. The soldiers that cornered Shinji under the stairs were under orders to kill him. Evidently, Shinji wasn't necessary for any of this. If anything, he was an obstruction for Seele because he was a human element in the plan. Hence the termination of the EVA pilots and staff.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:04 pm Monk Ed wrote: There is plenty of debate on whether or not Shinji was actually necessary. I fall very strongly in the "no" camp. To the extent that anyone really needed him it seems to be only because Yui and Rei forced everyone else into it: Yui refused any pilot but him, and Rei (as GNR) hijacked Seele's plans and handed the control (to some extent) to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:27 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: I imagine that if the JSSDF soldiers had any 'take alive' orders, it was for Gendo and Rei, if at that.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:47 pm Reichu wrote: Doubtful. Rei would be implicitly included with "Eva pilots" (no longer active, but that's basically what she is, as First Children), and Kiel's last words to Gendo were a direct threat ("...Then death is what you shall receive", or whatever it was).

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Jan 12, 2013 9:08 pm Seele wan't happy with Gendo by this point. If they wanted anybody in particular dead that wasn't an EVA pilot, I'm pretty certain that it would be him. Plus, Misato was pretty sure that Rei would be killed in EoE. Generally, she's pretty well informed, save for her lapse of data regarding Lilith (mistaking it for Adam).
summary:
[]: Why were the Nerv scientists killed?             Quote Post by Kaworu06 » Sun Dec 23, 2012 12:44 pm So, yeah, I watched EoE, and wondered why every Nerv tech-scientist was killed? I mean, not everyone knew about instrumentality or Rei-Tank. Didn't Seele want everyone to be one, where the flaws of me were fixed by the good of you or vice-versa? It's been a while since I watched EoE.

[]:        &nbsp

Article: Why were the Nerv scientists killed
 01, 2013 6:00 pm Seele was more interested in forcing Nerv's hand towards Third Impact than they were in taking hostages. Taking hostages takes time. Killing them is simply an easier (albeit messier) method. As to the debate over whether Shinji was necessary or not, I was divided on the issue until a few days ago, when I took two simple facts into account: 1. All offensive soldiers in Nerv were under Seele's control. 2. The soldiers that cornered Shinji under the stairs were under orders to kill him. Evidently, Shinji wasn't necessary for any of this. If anything, he was an obstruction for Seele because he was a human element in the plan. Hence the termination of the EVA pilots and staff.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:04 pm Monk Ed wrote: There is plenty of debate on whether or not Shinji was actually necessary. I fall very strongly in the "no" camp. To the extent that anyone really needed him it seems to be only because Yui and Rei forced everyone else into it: Yui refused any pilot but him, and Rei (as GNR) hijacked Seele's plans and handed the control (to some extent) to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:27 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: I imagine that if the JSSDF soldiers had any 'take alive' orders, it was for Gendo and Rei, if at that.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:47 pm Reichu wrote: Doubtful.
